pattern: Summing the x-coefficients and constants on each side reduces the equation to a*x = b, whose solvability is read off a and b.
hints: Split on '='; each side is a sum of signed terms that are either constants or multiples of x.
Accumulate two totals per side: the coefficient of x and the constant; then move everything to one side.

Let A be (left x-coeff minus right x-coeff) and B be (right const minus left const). If A is 0 and B is 0 there are infinite solutions; if A is 0 and B is not, no solution; otherwise x = B/A as an integer.

edge_inputs.description: terms on both sides, unique integer solution
bare x has implicit coefficient one

stress.description: random equations built from signed integer and x-terms on each side
stress.note: produces solvable, infinite, and no-solution equations; division uses integer arithmetic
